# S003 Run 024

## Metadata

- Scenario ID: `S003`
- Scenario: `S003-async-gpu-remote-node-image`
- Status: fixture-ready-pass
- Acceptance level: fixture-ready
- Coordinator: Codex
- Executed: June 6, 2026
- Workspace: `contract-sim/workspaces/S003/`
- Scope: full bounded fixture-readiness rerun after the validated post-Run 023
  C09 status-code patch and process guardrail.

## Purpose

Prove whether the post-Run 023 patch made S003 fixture-ready. All actors
received exact allowed file paths and were told not to use broad filesystem
discovery.

## Actor Verdicts

| Actor | Verdict | Summary |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `agent-user` | `fixture-ready` | Public context, public aliases, discovery/invoke/cancel/status/log/artifact/content fixtures, idempotency, cursors, and public binary fixture pass for declared S003 public branches. |
| `c03-service-catalog` | `fixture-ready` | Catalog list/detail/filter/route fixtures remain aligned and bounded. |
| `c04-agent-tool-gateway` | `fixture-ready` | Public projections, dependency fakes, public-safe links, auth/policy subjects, idempotency/errors, and artifact proxy pass. |
| `c05-async-job-service` | `fixture-ready` | Lifecycle, idempotency, claims, heartbeat, logs, completion, queued cancel, failure branches, and projection boundaries pass. |
| `c06-resource-lease-service` | `fixture-ready` | Lease lifecycle, local token facts, C06-owned audit, expiry, cancel, and deterministic timeout heartbeat events pass. |
| `c07-artifact-store` | `fixture-ready` | Upload, content, completion, read/list/policy-context, first-write validation, idempotency, and binary fixtures pass. |
| `c08-access-policy-and-secrets` | `fixture-ready` | Auth grammar, allow/deny decisions, supplied context, state-specific job cancel, pre-artifact registration, and secrets scope pass. |
| `c09-runtime-node-agent` | `fixture-ready` | Health/status/start/stop, `service_start_accepted` `202`, `service_running` `200`, start idempotency, bounded endpoint-auth facts, and provider boundary pass. |
| `c10-comfyui-provider` | `fixture-ready` | Provider health, invoke, dry-run, content handoff, validation, auth, backend unavailable, timeout, expired content, and binary fixtures pass. |
| `composition-runner` | `fixture-ready` | Claim-to-complete, independent C05/C06 lifetimes, provider unavailable/timeout, lease expiration, binary handoff, and audit exclusion pass. |

## Boundary Findings

- No actor reported needing sibling private state.
- All actors stayed within exact-path bounded packages.
- Public actor context remained public-only.
- C05 job claims and C06 resource leases remained independently modeled.
- C07 remained the durable artifact owner.
- C08 continued to use supplied context rather than inspecting C05/C07
  internals.
- C09 endpoint-auth facts remained local replay facts and did not make C09 the
  global policy owner.
- Runner assertions stayed out of C06-owned audit internals.

## Non-Blocking Notes

- `agent-user` has no machine-readable public error-case fixture. Error shape is
  documented in the public contract excerpt, and this is acceptable for the
  declared S003 success/cancel branches. Future public error scenarios should
  add machine-readable public error fixtures.
- C09 fixture README lists `service_running` twice. Both references point to
  the same intended running-status fixture and do not block replay. This can be
  cleaned up during a future documentation tidy pass.

## Outcome

Fixture-ready for the declared S003 scope. The current S003 bounded role-play
workspace has machine-readable fixture packages and local binary fixtures
sufficient to extract code-level contract tests for the covered branches.

This is not an implementation-ready claim. The next project step is to turn
the fixture-ready packages into implementation-facing test fixtures and build
work items.
